front: What is the formal definition of a function from set A to set B?
back: A function f from a set A to a set B is a relation from A to B with the property that each element of A is related to exactly one element of B. It is denoted as f: A → B.

front: What does it mean for a function f: A → B to be one-to-one (injective)?
back: A function is one-to-one (injective) if and only if for all a₁ and a₂ in A, if f(a₁) = f(a₂), then a₁ = a₂. This means no two different elements in the domain map to the same element in the codomain.

front: What is the key difference between a plain function and a one-to-one function?
back: A plain function requires that every input has exactly one output. A one-to-one function adds the stricter requirement that every output is the image of at most one input, ensuring uniqueness.

front: What is the horizontal line test and what does it determine?
back: The horizontal line test is a visual method used to determine if a function is one-to-one. If a horizontal line intersects the graph of the function at more than one point, then the function is not one-to-one.

front: Give an example of a function that is plain but NOT one-to-one.
back: The function f: ℝ → ℝ defined by f(x) = x² is a plain function, but it is not one-to-one because f(2) = 4 and f(-2) = 4, meaning two different inputs (2 and -2) map to the same output.
